2023-09-24.abstract: Karen Rodriguez was kidnapped by Mexico’s Zeta cartel. Her mother would stop at nothing to find out exactly what happened to her.

2023-09-24.title: Cash, Mules and Paid Protests: How a Fraudster Seized an Ethnic Enclave
2023-09-24.abstract: The war in Ukraine is intensifying longstanding frictions in a region of Moldova called Gagauzia. A Moldovan convicted of plundering his country’s banking system sees an opportunity.

2023-09-24.title: Clams Ruled This Town Until the Crabs Moved In
2023-09-24.abstract: Goro, on the Adriatic Sea, is famous for its clams — essential for the beloved spaghetti alle vongole. But an infestation of crabs is threatening the town’s cash crop.

2023-09-24.title: Why Are So Many Venezuelans Going to the United States?
2023-09-24.abstract: Unable to build safe or stable lives in other parts of South America, many people are making the perilous journey to the United States.

2023-09-25.title: Matteo Messina Denaro, Long-Sought Italian Mafia Boss, Dies at 61
2023-09-25.abstract: A high-ranking member of the Cosa Nostra, he was arrested in January after decades on the run. He was found through medical records related to his cancer treatment.

2023-09-25.title: Firefighters Respond to Strike on Odesa Hotel
2023-09-25.abstract: The hotel, which stood as a landmark in the Port of Odesa, was unoccupied, according to regional officials.

2023-09-25.title: In Romania, the Traumas of a Bloody Revolution Still Cast a Long Shadow
2023-09-25.abstract: People struggling for justice for family members killed in the 1989 uprising say that servants of the Ceausescu regime have blocked any reckoning with the past.

2023-09-25.title: A New Border Crossing: Americans Turn to Mexico for Abortions
2023-09-25.abstract: American women are seeking help from Mexico for abortions, crystallizing the shifting policies of two nations that once held vastly different positions on the procedure.

2023-09-25.title: Finland Raced to Join NATO. What Happens Next Is Complicated.
2023-09-25.abstract: After decades of going it alone in security issues, Finns are finding that life in a large alliance is complex, expensive and deeply political.

2023-09-26.title: In a Port City Severed From the Sea, Young Sailors Feel Adrift
2023-09-26.abstract: With Russia trying to maintain military control of the Black Sea, Odesa is disconnected from its waters — and its history.

2023-09-26.title: After Taking Away Critics’ Citizenship, a Country Takes Their Houses
2023-09-26.abstract: Nicaragua’s government has begun confiscating the homes of former political prisoners and dissidents forced into exile, just as the country did in the 1980s.

2023-09-26.title: Death Toll Rises After Explosion at Nagorno-Karabakh Fuel Depot
2023-09-26.abstract: The cause of the blast in the region of Azerbaijan where thousands have been fleeing for Armenia was not immediately clear.

2023-09-26.title: What to Know About 5 People Suspected of Spying for Russia in the U.K.
2023-09-26.abstract: Three men and two women, all citizens of Bulgaria, appeared via video in court on Tuesday after being charged with conspiracy to conduct espionage.

2023-09-26.title: Depleted Uranium Shells Could Help Ukraine. Critics Say It’s Not Worth the Risk.
2023-09-26.abstract: The material is denser than steel, and shells made from it can penetrate enemy tanks’ armor. But they leave behind radioactive dust that can be harmful.

2023-09-27.title: A Stunningly Sudden End to a Long, Bloody Conflict in the Caucasus
2023-09-27.abstract: After decades of wars and tense stalemates, almost no one saw it coming: Azerbaijan seized Nagorno-Karabakh from Armenian control seemingly overnight.

2023-09-27.title: Deadly Fire in Iraq Reduces Wedding Hall to Charred Debris
2023-09-27.abstract: Numerous witnesses said flares fired toward the ceiling as the bride and groom danced started the fire, which quickly engulfed the building.

2023-09-27.title: As Hundreds Were Celebrating a Wedding, It Turned Into an Inferno
2023-09-27.abstract: As the bride and groom danced, witnesses said, flares were set off at the venue in northern Iraq, starting a fire that spread with astonishing speed and killed more than 100 people.

2023-09-27.title: Years of Graft Doomed 2 Dams in Libya, Leaving Thousands in Muddy Graves
2023-09-27.abstract: Repair work was agreed but never finished and Derna paid the price. Experts say infrastructure projects have long been neglected, with officials focusing instead on lining their own pockets.

2023-09-27.title: American Soldier Who Crossed Into North Korea in July Is in U.S. Custody
2023-09-27.abstract: Pvt. Travis T. King dashed across the inter-Korean Demilitarized Zone in July. North Korea expelled him after finding him guilty of “illegally intruding” into its territory.

2023-09-28.title: Who’s Gaining Ground in Ukraine? This Year, No One.
2023-09-28.abstract: Although both sides have launched ambitious offensives, the front line has barely shifted. After 18 months of war, a breakthrough looks more difficult than ever.

2023-09-28.title: Sikh Separatism Is a Nonissue in India, Except as a Political Boogeyman
2023-09-28.abstract: India’s feud with Canada highlights how Prime Minister Narendra Modi has amplified a separatist threat that in reality is largely a diaspora illusion.

2023-09-28.title: Suspect in Killing of Baltimore Tech C.E.O. Pava LaPere Is Arrested
2023-09-28.abstract: The murder of Ms. LaPere, who founded EcoMap Technologies, shocked Baltimore’s business community.

2023-09-28.title: The Potty-Mouthed, Baby-Faced Japanese Racer Who Became a Cult Star
2023-09-28.abstract: The impish persona and insouciant attitude of the Formula 1 driver Yuki Tsunoda have overturned stereotypes. The next step? Showing he can keep up with rivals.

2023-09-28.title: M. S. Swaminathan, Scientist Who Helped Conquer Famine in India, Dies at 98
2023-09-28.abstract: Called the father of India’s Green Revolution, he served on agencies and boards around the world and developed a system of ecologically safe food production.

2023-09-29.title: Talking Peace in Sudan, the U.A.E. Secretly Fuels the Fight
2023-09-29.abstract: From a remote air base in Chad, the Emirates is giving arms and medical treatment to fighters on one side in Sudan’s worsening war, officials say.

2023-09-29.title: A U.S. Army hospital has quietly started admitting troops wounded in Ukraine.
2023-09-29.abstract: The Army’s Landstuhl Regional Medical Center has quietly started admitting Ukrainian Army soldiers who were wounded in combat, most of them American volunteers.

2023-09-29.title: Why Doesn’t Australia Have Better Trains?
2023-09-29.abstract: And a glimmer of high-speed hope.

2023-09-29.title: How Peter Thiel’s Palantir Pushed Toward the Heart of U.K. Health Care
2023-09-29.abstract: Palantir, the analytics company led by Peter Thiel, has courted N.H.S. England with pandemic help and assertive lobbying. Its big reward may be yet to come.

2023-09-29.title: Russian Forces Left This City a Year Ago. The Scars Remain.
2023-09-29.abstract: In Izium, in eastern Ukraine, the buildings are in ruins and services are spotty. Barely recovered, its residents worry that more mayhem may lie ahead.

2023-09-30.title: Maldives President Nears Defeat in Vote Overshadowed by India and China
2023-09-30.abstract: Mohamed Muizzu, the mayor of the capital city pushing for closer ties with China, was leading against the pro-India incumbent Ibrahim Mohamed Solih, initial results showed.

2023-09-30.title: Slovakia’s Election Could Echo in Ukraine. Here’s What to Expect.
2023-09-30.abstract: A leader who has railed against NATO and sanctions on Russia appears poised for a strong showing. That could threaten Europe’s unity on support for Ukraine.

2023-09-30.title: Where German Cars Falter, E-Bikes Gain in Power
2023-09-30.abstract: E-bikes sales in Germany have jumped, as drivers, suppliers and even automakers embrace their role in the future of transportation.

2023-09-30.title: In an Israeli Oasis, a Model for Peace, if Messy and Imperfect
2023-09-30.abstract: In a small village, Jews and Arabs have chosen to live side by side, share power and imagine a more hopeful future. But even here, the agonies of the conflict can’t be escaped entirely.

2023-09-30.title: On Anniversary of Illegal Annexation, Russia Strikes Ukrainian Region It Claims as Its Own
2023-09-30.abstract: As little territory changes hands on the battlefield, President Vladimir Putin of Russia and President Volodymyr Zelensky of Ukraine both signal that they are prepared to keep fighting.
